extraction.one_line_summary: MATTHEWS Michael won stage 16 after Sunweb executed pre-announced plan to split race on opening climb, then capitalized when Sky's crosswind attack at 20km eliminated rival sprinters.
tactical_patterns: Sunweb spent Matthews on opening climb work and intermediate sprint, yet he still won—suggests exceptional form outweighs energy conservation.
Sky's crosswind split at km-to-go 20 accidentally helped Matthews by eliminating Gaviria, showing GC team tactics can inadvertently favor opportunistic sprinters.
Quick-Step spread too thin protecting Kittel's green jersey and supporting Martin simultaneously, losing both battles—classic overcommitment error.
ARU Fabio showed tactical awareness following Sky rotation in crosswinds while Martin missed his teammates' wheels, revealing positioning discipline separates top-10 finishers.

rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: positive: Won after doing opening climb pulls and intermediate sprint work, indicating form peak allows tactical energy expenditure others cannot match.
rider_signals.KITTEL Marcel: negative: Lost 12 minutes and 20 green jersey points despite Quick-Step teammates; suggests team unable to protect him on semi-mountainous windy stages.
rider_signals.MARTIN Dan: negative: Lost 50 seconds by missing teammate wheels before Sky crosswind attack at km 20, showing positioning lapses under pressure.
rider_signals.ARU Fabio: positive: Demonstrated heads-up positioning by following Sky rotation in crosswinds, suggesting improved tactical awareness compared to earlier grand tours.
rider_signals.BUCHMANN Emanuel: neutral: Bridged 2-minute gap solo then got dropped in crosswinds, showing climbing strength insufficient without team support in windy finales.
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: positive: Team Sky executed premeditated crosswind attack at exact km 180 as publicly announced, revealing psychological dominance when rivals cannot counter known tactics.
